title: Moving During the Enkutatash (Ethiopian New Year) Rush: Booking Early to Secure Your Date
excerpt: Enkutatash moving rush: Ethiopian New Year bookings, peak season trucks, secure move date early, Meskerem relocation planning Addis Ababa.
content:
Moving during the Enkutatash (Ethiopian New Year) rush: booking early to secure your date

Enkutatash brings yellow daisies, new year joy, and moving truck scarcity—families time lease turns to Meskerem, students return, diaspora visits, and every reputable mover books solid two to three weeks out. Waiting until Pagumen week is lottery with your deposit on the line.

This guide helps you secure moving dates during Enkutatash rush.

Why Enkutatash compresses capacity

  • Lease cycles align to new year calendar psychology.
  • Diaspora households coordinate visits and relocations.
  • Commercial offices refresh after budget year.
  • Rain taper improves access—more moves same trucks.
  • Holiday events compete for crew availability.

Booking timeline

T-4 weeks: Survey and written quote.
T-3 weeks: Sign contract and deposit—date holds.
T-1 week: Confirm plate, crew, elevator book.
Day before: Weather and traffic check with dispatcher.

Flex pricing reality

Peak week may carry premium—book shoulder week before or after Enkutatash if flexible; mid-week Tuesday-Wednesday still helps traffic side.

Avoid delala last-minute

Rush week delala hikes worst—professional held date beats frantic gate negotiation.

Prep before crew arrives

Declutter complete; iddir street events noted; gift traffic near family homes—build buffer in start time.

Active Movers Enkutatash calendar

Book Meskerem dates in Nehase—August inquiry for September move is prudent.

Conclusion

Enkutatash moving rush rewards early booking—survey in Pagumen, deposit in hand, date confirmed before yellow flowers peak. New year deserves new home, not new stress from no truck.
author: Active Movers Team
